preamble: The shift from the open-mid back unrounded vowel [ʌ] to the front-closing diphthong [ei] involves fronting and diphthongization. This is common in vowel shifts where central/back vowels are fronted and subsequently diphthongized.

examples.note: Fronting and raising towards a high-front glide.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
